name: Middle Welsh

ancestors.notes: Descent per Glottolog classification of Middle Welsh (midd1363).

notes: Skeleton. Brittonic (Insular Celtic) medieval stage written in the Latin alphabet. Grapheme map covers the vowels a e i o u w y, the characteristic digraphs ll [ɬ], rh, dd [ð], ff, ch, th, ng, and the plain consonants; Middle Welsh spelling was not standardised and freely used k and v where the modern language writes c and f. Vowel length, the two phonetic values of y, and consonant mutation triggered by context are not recoverable from the spelling and are left unencoded, raising the PER floor honestly.

sources.author: Hammarström, H. et al. (Glottolog 5.0)
sources.title: Glottolog: Middle Welsh (midd1363)
sources.publisher: Max Planck Institute for Evolutionary Anthropology
